publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, printed materials, publications, research materials

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Research associate, Cornell University; ornithologist at the American Museum of Natural History. Brand was born in 1889 and died in 1940.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Brand's correspondence (1928-40) with Arthur A. Allen, Peter Paul Kellogg, Herbert Sanborn of Vanderbilt University, Dr. Frank E. Lutz, curator of the Museum, and others, mainly concerning his pioneering work recording the songs and calls of American wild birds and his ornithological writing; drafts and reprints of his articles and speeches (1930-38); typescript notes (1930-39) titled "Bird Diaries" and "Bird Hearing Experiment"; typescripts and photographs (one bound volume) describing the Cornell-American Museum of Natural History Ornithological Expedition of 1935, as well as letters from Allen and Kellogg to Brand during and after the expedition; correspondence of Herbert J. Seligmann with Brand's widow, Ernestine I. Brand, and others concerning his proposed biography of Brand and drafts of the first four chapters of this book; other biographical data, including a scrapbook of clippings concerning Brand; photographs, correspondence, and printed items concerning ornithological meetings; and lists of his publications. Includes a leather-bound book entitled "Hunting with a Mike" that was issued in 1935 to the members of the Cornell sound expedition, with a photographic and text account of the trip. The collection also includes 48 phonograph recordings of birds and frogs made Brand including two put out by the American Foundation for the Blind. Also, thesis, Recording Bird Songs, 1932.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIAL
relatedmaterial.p: The Peter Paul Kellogg papers, #21-18-893, contain recordings made by Brand.

The James Taylor Tanner papers, #21-18-1362 and the Arthur Augustus Allen papers, #21-18-1255 contain additional materials about the Laboratory of Ornithology and the 1935 Cornell-American Museum of Natural History Ornithological Expedition.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Albert Rich Brand papers, #21-18-899. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Manuscripts of articles by Brand - B-H
Manuscripts of Articles by Brand - H-S
Correspondence used by Seligmann in his book about Brand
Chapter I; first draft - Biography of Albert R. Brand by H.J. Seligmann
Chapter II; first draft - Biography of Albert R. Brand by H.J. Seligmann
Chapter III; first draft - Biography of Albert R. Brand by H.J. Seligmann
Chapter IV; first draft - Biography of Albert R. Brand by H.J. Seligmann
Chapter I; second draft - Biography of Albert R. Brand by H.J. Seligmann
Chapter II; second draft - Biography of Albert R. Brand by H.J. Seligmann
Chapter III; second draft - Biography of Albert R. Brand by H.J. Seligmann
Chapter IV; second draft - Biography of Albert R. Brand by H.J. Seligmann
Extra Reprints by Albert R. Brand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscripts of radio talks and articles as well as a reprint by Arthur Allen

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Laboratory of Ornithology Newsletters that mention Brand, a reprint by Peter Paul Kellogg and a report on the recordings of wild birds by Brand.

did.unittitle: Talks and speeches by Albert R. Brand
Talks and speeches by Albert R. Brand
The History of American Ornithology Before Audubon by Elsa G. Allen
Special articles about and by Brand
A memoir of American Ornithologists Union, Charleston, N.C.
Notice of admission to Cornell, etc.
Special letters relating to Albert R. Brand and bird recording
Information gathered by Brand for the National Cyclopedia of American Biography

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Itinerary, expenses, correspondence and programs for events

did.unittitle: "An Informal Report of the First Year's Work in an Experiment in Recording the Songs of Wild Birds" - Thesis by Brand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mainly taken on recording trips with Arthur Allen. Photos include Arthur Allen, Peter Paul Kellogg, Elsa Guerdrum Allen, Herbert Friedman, and Charles Brand

did.unittitle: Hunting with a Mike by Albert R. Brand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound book of the Cornell/Museum of Natural History expedition. Includes photos of the people involved with the expedition and their activities, including George Miksch Sutton, George Lowery, J. J. Kuhn, and others. Also includes photos of the ivory billed wood pecker.

did.unittitle: 50th Anniversery American Ornithologists' Union, Museum of Natural History, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Date and title are engraved in the center of the record.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Date and title are engraved in the center of the record.

did.unittitle: 3A: Eastern Meadowlark, Western Meadowlark, Dickcissel, White-eyed Vireo, B.C. Chickadee, Nighthawk
3A (Second Best): Eastern Meadowlark, Western Meadowlark, Dickcissel, White-eyed Vireo, B.C. Chickadee, Nighthawk
3B (1st Set): House Finch, Purple Finch, Orchard Oriole, Warbling Vireo, (Flicker Call), Red-eyed Vireo, Blue-headed Vireo, Yellow-throated Vireo
3B (2nd Set): House Finch, Purple Finch, Orchard Oriole, Warbling Vireo, (Flicker Call), Red-eyed Vireo, Blue-headed Vireo, Yellow-throated Vireo
3B (Badly Scratched): House Finch, Purple Finch, Orchard Oriole, Warbling Vireo, (Flicker Call), Red-eyed Vireo, Blue-headed Vireo, Yellow-throated Vireo
4A (1st Set): Canada Warbler, Ruby-crowned Kinglet, Hooded Warbler, Kentucky Warbler, Ovenbird, Piliated Woodpecker, Barred Owl
4A (2nd Best): Canada Warbler, Ruby-crowned Kinglet, Hooded Warbler, Kentucky Warbler, Ovenbird, Piliated Woodpecker, Barred Owl
4B: Spring Morning, Bull Frog, Blue Jay, Red-wing Blackbird, Spring Peepers, Pied-billed Grebe, Crested Flycatcher, Downy Woodpecker, Yellow-billed Sapsucker, Flicker
4B (Second Best): Spring Morning, Bull Frog, Blue Jay, Red-wing Blackbird, Spring Peepers, Pied-billed Grebe, Crested Flycatcher, Downy Woodpecker, Yellow-billed Sapsucker, Flicker
5A: Chirping Sparrow, Swamp Sparrow, Red-winged Blackbird, Pine Woods Sparrow, Bluebird, Towhee, Spotted Towhee, Chuck-will's Widow
5A: Chirping Sparrow, Swamp Sparrow, Red-winged Blackbird, Pine Woods Sparrow, Bluebird, Towhee, Spotted Towhee, Chuck-will's Widow

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "To Paul with my compliments, yes compliments!" Albert
